case_title: MARC LANGLOIS et al VS. CHARLES HINGER et al

docket_entries.description: LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANTS CHARLES HINGER, AND JAY BEATTY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT FOR PARTITION AND ACCOUNTING OF REAL PROPERTY IS OVERRULED WITH 10 DAYS TO ANSWER. ORDER WAS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189

LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANTS CHARLES HINGER, AND JAY BEATTY'S CROSS-MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT DOES NOT NECESSARILY PRECLUDE PLAINTIFF FROM SEEKING PARTITION BASED ON NEW CIRCUMSTANCES DIFFERENT FROM THOSE EXISTING WHEN THE FIRST CASE WAS DISMISSED. THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT IN ALLOWING THE SALE OF A TIC INTEREST, DID NOT PROVIDE THAT NO OTHER REMEDY COULD EVEN BE SOUGHT. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189

LAW AND MOTION 301, NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFFS MARC LANGLOIS AND CRAIG MATHIS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS CONTINUED TO OCTOBER 29, 2009 ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION. JUDGE: PAUL J. ALVARADO, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR # 8189 301/PHA

LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFFS MARC LANGLOIS, AND CRAIG MATHIS' MOTION FOR INTERLOCUTORY SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE COURT CANNOT DETERMINE AS A MATTER OF LAW BASED ON THE UNDISPUTED FACTS IN PLAINTIFF'S SEPARATE STATEMENT THAT PARTITION AND SALE OF THE WHOLE PROPERTY IS THE MOST EQUITABLE OUTCOME NECESSARILY. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189
